articles.title: Crude shock! Oil prices poised for biggest ever single-day gain after 29% rally
articles.summary: Brent crude surged nearly 29% on Monday to cross $100 per barrel, heading for its biggest single-day gain on record after the Strait of Hormuz remained shut amid the escalating Iran–Israel–US conflict. The disruption to a key global oil transit route has sparked fears of a severe supply shock and rising inflation.

articles.title: SBI shareholders lose Rs 62,350 crore as stock falls nearly 6% in market sell-off
articles.summary: State Bank of India shares plunged 5.60%, erasing nearly Rs 62,352 crore in market value amid a broader market sell-off driven by geopolitical tensions.  The bank's valuation dipped below Rs 10 lakh crore. Despite this, SBI's financial performance remains strong, with a significant net profit increase in the December quarter.

articles.title: IOCL, HPCL, BPCL tumble up to 9% on UBS downgrade amid crude surge; check targets
articles.summary: Indian Oil, HPCL, and BPCL stocks saw sharp declines on Monday. International brokerage UBS downgraded these companies citing concerns over rising crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions. This uncertainty impacts their earnings. UBS also revised target prices downwards for all three. The situation draws parallels with past oil market disruptions.

articles.title: Enterprise agentic AI requires a process layer most companies haven’t built
articles.summary: Presented by Celonis85% of enterprises want to become agentic within three years — yet 76% admit their operations can’t support it. According to the Celonis 2026 Process Optimization Report, based on a survey of more than 1,600 global business leaders, organizations are aggressively pursuing AI-driven transformation. Yet most acknowledge that the foundational work — modernizing workflows, reducing process friction, and building operational resilience — remains unfinished. The ambition is clear.

articles.title: Global Markets | Strong wage data in Japan fuels bets on more BOJ tightening

articles.summary: Google senior AI product manager Shubham Saboo has turned one of the thorniest problems in agent design into an open-source engineering exercise: persistent memory.This week, he published an open-source “Always On Memory Agent” on the official Google Cloud Platform Github page under a permissive MIT License, allowing for commercial usage.It was built with Google's Agent Development Kit, or ADK introduced last Spring in 2025, and Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, a low-cost model Google introduced on March

articles.title: Trump Aides Remain Bullish Despite Rising Unemployment and Gas Prices
articles.summary: The president’s economic advisers insist that any signs of strains are temporary, as they look to sell the president’s agenda entering the midterms.

articles.title: From 1922: Nellie Bly, Journalist, Dies of Pneumonia
articles.summary: She was best known for her trip around the world in 1889, which she completed in 72 days 6 hours 11 minutes.

articles.title: When AI Companies Go to War, Safety Gets Left Behind
articles.summary: We were promised AI regulation and a race to the top. Now, we’re arguing about killer robots.

articles.title: From 2001: Beate Uhse, 81, Entrepreneur in the Business of Erotic Goods
articles.summary: She developed Europe’s biggest emporium of erotic goods and opened a three-story sex museum in Germany.

articles.title: From 2019: Agnès Varda, Influential French New Wave Filmmaker, Dies at 90
articles.summary: She was closely associated with the film movement known as the New Wave, although her reimagining of cinematic conventions predated it.

articles.title: Integrity Viking Funds signs for FIS Investment Accounting Manager
articles.summary: Global financial technology leader FIS today announced that Integrity Viking Funds has selected FIS Investment Accounting Manager to modernize its investment accounting infrastructure.

articles.title: ICBA adds Jack Henry's Financial Crimes Defender to preferred service provider programme
articles.summary: Jack Henry (Nasdaq: JKHY) announced today that Jack Henry Financial Crimes Defender has been added to the Independent Community Bankers of America® (ICBA) Preferred Service Provider (PSP) program.

articles.title: West Virginia bank director quits over executive pay concerns
articles.summary: Glen Herrick, who resigned from MVB’s board, cited a “lack of alignment between pay and performance” and a “lack of strategic focus on core profitability.”

articles.title: From 1973: Mrs. Marjorie Merriweather Post Is Dead at 86
articles.summary: The owner of General Foods Corporation, she was a generous philanthropist whose great wealth also allowed her to entertain lavishly and live regally.

articles.title: From 2004: Estée Lauder, Pursuer of Beauty and Cosmetics Titan, Dies at 97
articles.summary: Her company, known for its creams, powders, ointments and perfumes, was estimated to be worth about $5 billion when it went public in 1995.

articles.title: From 2004: Julia Child, the French Chef for a Jell-O Nation, Dies at 91
articles.summary: She was a towering figure on the culinary front for more than 40 years, turning the art of French cooking into prime-time television entertainment.

articles.title: Western Alliance sues Jefferies over unpaid debt tied to First Brands
articles.summary: The Phoenix, Arizona-based lender’s CEO called the investment bank’s decision to cease payments “shocking.” Jefferies said the bank’s lawsuit is “without merit.”

articles.title: Morgan Stanley partner Zerohash applies for OCC trust charter
articles.summary: The application comes amid fierce blowback from a bank trade group leader who criticized the OCC’s “unfettered discretion” on which firms get charters and for which activities.

articles.title: TransUnion targets AI-driven credit intelligence with Google Cloud
articles.summary: TransUnion (NYSE: TRU) today announced its AI Analytics Orchestrator Agent, a next-generation capability built on its OneTru™ solution enablement platform. Leveraging Google’s Gemini models, the new Agent is designed to power advanced analytics within TransUnion’s TruIQ® solutions suite.

articles.title: One in seven UK high street shops went cashless in last year
articles.summary: The UK's high streets are increasingly going cashless, according to research which shows that 14% of shops have stopped accepting notes and coins in the last 12 months.

articles.title: Iran war could ultimately weaken US and benefit China
articles.summary:
The widening conflict between the United States and Iran is already reshaping the Middle East’s geopolitical landscape. Supporters of Washington’s military campaign argue that weakening Tehran will restore deterrence and protect regional allies. Yet the broader strategic consequences of the war may ultimately undermine American power rather than strengthen it. Military campaigns can degrade an […]
The post Iran war could ultimately weaken US and benefit China appeared first on Asia Times.

articles.title: Trump wants U.S. Navy to escort tankers through the Gulf. Why that plan may not work
articles.summary: A backup of oil tankers in the Persian Gulf is disrupting supplies, and will only get worse the longer the Strait of Hormuz is closed.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley to axe 2,500 staff across divisions
articles.summary: Banking giant Morgan Stanley is slashing 3% of its workforce across divisions due to shifting business priorities.

articles.title: Lloyds to sell customer data, cut IT costs to become “the UK’s biggest fintech”
articles.summary: Internal documents reveal Lloyds Bank’s aim to reduce technology costs by 35% by selling customer data and automating governance checks.

articles.title: Iran war rocks Indian Ocean, torpedoing India’s strategic autonomy
articles.summary:
The US-Iran war has reached the Indian Ocean. The sinking of the Iranian navy frigate IRIS Dena, reportedly struck by a US submarine about 40 nautical miles south of Galle, Sri Lanka, has pushed the confrontation far beyond its traditional arena in the Persian Gulf. Iranian naval sources and regional security analysts say the vessel […]
The post Iran war rocks Indian Ocean, torpedoing India’s strategic autonomy appeared first on Asia Times.

articles.title: Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs
articles.summary: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”

articles.title: The War on Iran Puts Global Chip Supplies and AI Expansion at Risk
articles.summary: From helium extraction in Qatar to shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz, the semiconductor industry depends on fragile links across the Gulf. Escalation could ripple through global chip production.

articles.title: China dials down growth ambitions with decades-low target. Here's why
articles.summary: China has set its lowest growth target in decades, acknowledging domestic challenges and pointing to global uncertainty.

articles.title: Why the world's top-performing stock market in 2025 is seeing historic volatility
articles.summary: South Korea's stock market has swung wildly in recent days, underscoring how the world's best-performing equities market last year can also be among its most volatile.

articles.title: Robinhood takes on Amex with $695-a-year platinum credit card
articles.summary: Fintech Robinhood is taking on American Express with a $695-a-year credit card plated with real platinum.

articles.title: Citi and HSBC invest in trading tech player Adaptive
articles.summary: Citi and HSBC have made strategic investments in custom trading technology specialist Adaptive. Terms were not disclosed.

articles.title: Western automakers outsourced supply chains for decades — now Chinese rivals have the cost edge
articles.summary: A recent Rhodium Group report found that structural factors outweigh the effects of state subsidies on the profit margins of Chinese electric vehicle companies.

articles.title: OpenAI Had Banned Military Use. The Pentagon Tested Its Models Through Microsoft Anyway
articles.summary: Sources allege the Defense Department experimented with Microsoft’s version of OpenAI technology before the ChatGPT-maker lifted its prohibition on military applications.

articles.title: ByteDance’s AI Ambitions Are Being Hampered by Compute Restraints and Copyright Concerns
articles.summary: ByteDance’s new Seedance 2.0 AI video model seemed unstoppable—until heavy demand strained the company’s compute capacity and copyright complaints began piling up.

articles.title: States led by New York sue to block Trump's latest tariffs, calling them an illegal end run around Supreme Court
articles.summary: The move from the state AGs — part of the successful effort to block Trump's original tariffs — adds to the ongoing uncertainty created by his tariff policies.

articles.title: Revolut applies for OCC charter
articles.summary: Cetin Duransoy has been named the fintech’s U.S. CEO, succeeding Sid Jajodia, who is now global chief banking officer. Jajodia said in October that a charter would give Revolut “a seat at the table” with regulators.

articles.title: Wells Fargo 2018 enforcement action terminated
articles.summary: The bank has sufficiently demonstrated improvements in governance and risk management since its 2016 fake-accounts scandal, the Federal Reserve said Thursday.

articles.title: Apple Blocks US Users From Downloading ByteDance’s Chinese Apps
articles.summary: In January, after TikTok announced a deal to transfer its US operations, Apple began blocking people in the US from downloading or updating ByteDance apps designed for the Chinese market.

articles.title: NYSE owner invests in crypto exchange OKX
articles.summary: NYSE parent Intercontinental Exchange has invested in OKX in a deal that values the cryptocurrency exchange at $25 billion. Terms were not disclosed.

articles.title: Capital One cuts more than 1,100 Discover employees
articles.summary: Numbers shared with the state of Illinois indicate 1,075 people will be let go in May, and another 81 on June 1.

articles.title: Mastercard unveils trust layer for agentic commerce
articles.summary: Mastercard has taken aim at one of the biggest emerging issues in the agentic commerce goldrush, signing up big name partners for a new open, standards-based layer for trust.

articles.title: Revolut files for US banking licence
articles.summary: Revolut has filed for a US banking licence and appointed a former Visa executive as its US CEO.

articles.title: Payment processor Silverflow raises $40m
articles.summary: Dutch cloud-native payment processing company Silverflow has closed a $40 million Series B funding round led by Picus Capital.

articles.title: Inside India newsletter: Energy, airlines and now over $50 billion in remittances to India at risk as Middle East conflict deepens
articles.summary: India can't seem to escape from the fallout of the escalating conflict in the Middle East. After energy and aviation, remittances could be the latest worry.

articles.title: Latin American neobank Ualá raises $195 million at $3.2 billion valuation
articles.summary: Latin American neobank Ualá has raised $195 million in equity financing led by the strategic investment arm of Allianz Group.

articles.title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
articles.summary: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.

articles.title: Kraken receives Fed master account, in a first for crypto
articles.summary: Access to the Federal Reserve’s payment system has long been a dream for cryptocurrency firms, including Custodia Bank, which has sued the central bank in pursuit of access.

articles.title: OCC’s proposed threshold change divides bank trade groups
articles.summary: Community lenders warned that boosting an asset threshold to $700 billion from $50 billion would go “far beyond what is appropriate or safe.” But large banks want the heightened standards scrapped.

articles.title: Citi pushes client co-creation in e-commerce
articles.summary: The bank has endeavored to work “hand in glove” with fintechs, rather than build tools thinking it will beat them, an executive said. “We want them to actually use the platform.”
